rules.title: Process before outcome
rules.principle: A trade idea is stronger when the next action is pre-decided before emotion or price movement forces a reaction.
rules.action: Before buying, convert the idea into if-this-then-that rules and do not increase size until the plan is explicit.
rules.caution: A good-looking setup without a defined action plan can still lead to impulsive execution.

rules.title: Accept risk and treat loss as feedback
rules.principle: Losses are part of the process; the useful question is whether the defined loss is affordable and informative.
rules.action: Size the position so being wrong is survivable and reviewable instead of emotionally damaging.
rules.caution: Avoid averaging down or revenge trading to erase the feeling of a loss.
rules.chapter: Accept risk and treat loss as feedback

rules.title: Impulse and temptation control
rules.principle: The urge to act quickly is not evidence. A clean setup should survive a slower, more deliberate check.
rules.action: If the buy reason is excitement, FOMO, a hot tip, or revenge, downgrade the verdict until objective evidence confirms.
rules.caution: Do not chase price just to avoid the discomfort of missing a move.

rules.title: Facts over bias
rules.principle: A decision improves when it separates observable evidence from stories, hindsight, and crowd comfort.
rules.action: Force the app to show both positives and cautions before accepting the verdict.
rules.caution: Do not upgrade confidence only because the idea feels obvious after a price move.

rules.title: Flexibility and standing aside
rules.principle: A good process allows waiting. Not buying is an active decision when evidence or reward/risk is incomplete.
rules.action: Use watchlist verdicts as a valid output instead of forcing every analysed ticker into a buy or sell.
rules.caution: Do not convert patience into paralysis; define what evidence would change the decision.

rules.title: Stress and resilience filter
rules.principle: Decision quality falls when stress is high. Smaller size and clearer rules reduce the chance of emotional decisions.
rules.action: When uncertain or stressed, use starter size, wait for confirmation, or postpone the trade.
rules.caution: Do not use the app to justify action when your real problem is pressure to recover quickly.

rules.title: Capital size matters
rules.principle: Position size should follow risk capacity, not confidence alone.
rules.action: Keep suggested quantity tied to max loss and liquidity; reduce size if the setup is thinly traded or volatile.
rules.caution: Do not use the full capital amount simply because it is available.

rules.title: Automobile operating lens
rules.principle: Auto companies need volume, realization, product mix, capacity, inventory, and leverage checks before the ratio picture is trusted.
rules.action: For auto names, add volume and mix commentary before increasing conviction.
rules.caution: Revenue growth can be misleading if it comes with inventory build-up or margin pressure.

rules.title: Banking asset-quality lens
rules.principle: Banks and lenders must be judged through credit quality, capital strength, margins, and provisioning, not ordinary industrial debt ratios.
rules.action: For lenders, downgrade confidence if asset-quality and capital data are missing.
rules.caution: Cheap valuation can be a trap when credit losses are rising.

rules.title: IT services margin and client lens
rules.principle: IT analysis should include client concentration, deal wins, margin trend, currency exposure, and employee costs.
rules.action: For IT names, prefer confirmation from margins and order/deal commentary, not only P/E.
rules.caution: A cash-rich balance sheet does not offset weak growth visibility.
rules.chapter: IT services margin and client lens

rules.title: Cyclical cost and capacity lens
rules.principle: Cyclical sectors need cost, spread, capacity, and debt checks because earnings can turn quickly across the cycle.
rules.action: For cyclical names, reduce confidence unless margins and balance sheet are checked across the cycle.
rules.caution: Peak earnings can make P/E look deceptively cheap.

rules.title: Consumer, hotel, and retail unit economics
rules.principle: Hotel and retail quality comes from unit economics: occupancy/pricing for hotels and store productivity/inventory for retail.
rules.action: Use unit-economics metrics before accepting growth as high quality.
rules.caution: Fast expansion can hide weak store economics or debt pressure.
rules.chapter: Consumer, hotel, and retail unit economics

rules.title: Real-estate cash-flow lens
rules.principle: Real-estate analysis should separate bookings from cash collections and track debt, inventory, and execution.
rules.action: For developers, require cash collection and debt evidence before trusting accounting profits.
rules.caution: Reported sales can be less useful than cash-flow timing and project delivery.

rules.title: Separate retirement-goal money from trade capital
rules.principle: Retirement and tax-planning capital should be protected from short-term trading decisions.
rules.action: Treat the capital input as deployable market capital only; do not include retirement or emergency money.
rules.caution: A good stock idea is still unsuitable if it risks money assigned to a non-negotiable life goal.
rules.chapter: Separate retirement-goal money from trade capital

rules.title: Asset allocation and horizon discipline
rules.principle: Long-term wealth plans need allocation discipline, periodic investing, and age/horizon awareness.
rules.action: If the user is investing for long goals, frame the stock as part of allocation rather than a standalone bet.
rules.caution: Aggressive concentration is weaker when the money belongs to a retirement allocation.

rules.title: Liquidity, lock-in, and tax clarity
rules.principle: Tax benefits and lock-in rules should not be confused with tradable liquidity.
rules.action: When capital may be goal-linked, caution the user to verify liquidity and tax consequences first.
rules.caution: Do not sell or buy equities to meet a tax idea without checking cash-flow needs.

rules.title: SSE instrument type filter
rules.principle: SSE-related securities may have social-impact objectives and different return/liquidity expectations from ordinary equities.
rules.action: Before applying normal buy/sell/hold logic, verify whether the instrument is an ordinary listed equity.
rules.caution: Do not rely on chart signals alone for a social-impact or non-standard fundraising instrument.

rules.title: ZCZP return caution
rules.principle: ZCZP and donation-like instruments should not be analysed like equity return opportunities.
rules.action: If ZCZP language appears, switch the verdict from trading analysis to suitability/donation-style due diligence.
rules.caution: Normal valuation ratios and candle patterns may be irrelevant for zero-coupon zero-principal structures.
